title: The Parallel
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Parallel}}{{VoidedTheme}}{{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family:Nunito}}__NOTOC__ '''The Parallel''' is a poorly understood anomalous level that near-perfectly replicates the Backrooms as a whole, but with some differences of varying magnitudes.
[[File:ParallelLevel59.jpg|300px|Ethereal mall|link=]]
An image of the '''Parallel''' version of
[[Level 59]].
==Description== Rather than being a proper individual level, the '''Parallel''' is a series of numerous other 'levels' — designated as sub-levels of the main level — that resemble those of the rest of the Backrooms at large. It is believed that every single level — including positives and anomalous — in the rest of the Backrooms is replicated within the '''Parallel''', though this cannot be confirmed. However, an extremely small portion of wanderers who have been to the '''Parallel''' and made it back out have reported that they witnessed certain replicated levels changing or being destroyed at the same time as these changes occurred at their original counterparts, indicating that the '''Parallel''' does, in fact, actively imitate the rest of the Backrooms' current state. The materials that make up the '''Parallel''' are almost ghostly in appearance, swirling and shifting slowly and subtly; moreover, they are also notably slightly desaturated in color. However, they still retain most of their regular properties and are no more translucent or transparent than they would otherwise be. It is unknown whether the physical materials that make up the '''Parallel''' are different versions of the ones they supposedly imitate, are all one type of unidentified material capable of mimicking the properties of other types, or are simply the same as their out-of-level types and are surrounded by an unknown anomaly that causes them to possess their unique properties. Regardless, this strange property may easily cause disorientation among wanderers in the '''Parallel''' due to it applying to every solid and liquid surface within the level, except for wanderers. After a wanderer has been in the '''Parallel''' for around 5-8 hours, their mental state will begin to anomalously deteriorate over time. The early symptoms of this phenomenon are trouble thinking clearly and a slight lack of coordination. Soon after, the victim's memory will start to become foggy and their emotions and senses will begin to dull. Eventually, it will have escalated to the point where the wanderer no longer displays any signs of intelligence or self-awareness. This entire process takes approximately 8 hours on average, ending when the phenomenon finally destroys the last of the victim's brain processes entirely, killing them. Unfortunately, [[Object 1]] cannot slow nor prevent the phenomenon. All reports of long-dead corpses discovered in the '''Parallel''' — of which there are very few due to the level's vastness — include said corpses being in various stages of being absorbed into the level. Witnesses stated that these corpses looked as if they were "melting" or "fusing" into whatever surface they were touching, with the ones in the apparently later stages of being absorbed allegedly appearing as relatively smaller and disfigured versions of what they were before and somewhat resembling the material(s) they were being absorbed into. It has been theorized that this grotesque phenomenon is the result of the '''Parallel''' itself 'feeding' on the corpses of wanderers, indicating that the level possesses its own sentience, although this is unlikely. Nevertheless, it has also been posited that the cognitive deterioration that wanderers in the '''Parallel''' often experience is also consciously caused by the level.
[[File:ParallelLevel20.jpg|300px|Ghostly warehouse|link=]]
An image of [[Level 20]]'s '''Parallel'''
version.
Travel between the sub-levels of the '''Parallel''' is much easier than traveling between levels in the rest of the Backrooms, although it is often not a matter of choice on the traveler's part. Transitions between the sub-levels occur relatively frequently, meaning that any wanderer in the main level will likely be transported to a different sub-level in the '''Parallel''' after an extended period. Moreover, when one initially arrives in the '''Parallel''', it is believed that one is no more likely to arrive at any of the level's sub-levels in particular over any other one. Despite this, it has been theorized that replicated anomalous levels are inherently much rarer to initially encounter. As the '''Parallel''' is most likely still mostly unexplored, considering only a relatively small number of its sub-levels have been officially discovered so far, there is likely much that is still not known about the level. Nonetheless, there is probably not a replica of the level itself within the '''Parallel''', since this would be indicative of a paradox. ===The Whispering Void=== {{Fullscreen|num=1|show='''> Open Location File'''|hide='''Close Location File'''|padding=8rem 25%|content= {{Background|file=The void.png}}
The
Whispering Void


{{Quote|In this Void, wanderers are not afforded the blissful silence of the [[The Void|all-surrounding one]]. Instead, they are plagued by constant, disembodied whispers that will quickly drive them into the depths of madness.|Unknown}} '''The Whispering Void''' acts as the [[The Void|Void]] of the '''Parallel''', seemingly functioning the same way as the regular Void. Many have speculated that the '''Whispering Void''' alone is the true '''Parallel''', thus explaining how it supposedly connects all of the level's sub-levels. However, others speculate that it is merely a relatively small part of the Void that acts slightly differently from the rest of the Void, which would arguably better explain the apparent connection of all of the '''Parallel''''s sub-levels. What is known for certain regarding the '''Whispering Void''' is that it can be used as a method of travel between sub-levels of the '''Parallel''', just as the Void can be used for inter-level travel in the rest of the Backrooms. However, whether this applies to every sub-level of the '''Parallel''' is currently unknown, as travel through the '''Whispering Void''' has not yet been nearly sufficiently documented. Notably, one does not pass through the '''Whispering Void''' during transitions between the sub-levels, at least not in any perceptible way. The only other differing property from the Void that the '''Whispering Void''' possesses aside from its evident link to the '''Parallel''' is the constant barrage of whispers that assault any wanderer in its pitch-black expanse. These whispers seem to come from all directions around any such wanderer and last for the entire duration of their stay, beginning as soon as one arrives and only ceasing when — and if — one manages to leave. Moreover, the voices have been reported to be unusually loud, unintelligible, and sounding more distorted and monstrous than regular human voices. It is uncertain whether some unknown entity or entities produce these whispers. Nonetheless, it is known that the whispers are extremely fear- and anxiety-inducing — likely mostly as a result of a mysterious cognitive anomaly — and that they will quickly drive one to insanity if one is trapped within the '''Whispering Void''' for long enough.
}} ==Entities== No entities have been officially reported to reside within the '''Parallel'''. Nonetheless, it remains unknown if the whispers in the Whispering Void are caused by a certain type of entity or if the '''Parallel''' itself is an entity. ==Colonies & Outposts== Due to the unstable and long-term lethal nature of the '''Parallel''', establishing any colonies or outposts in the level is infeasible. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== So far, the only known entrance to the '''Parallel''' is by falling through [[The Void]], though the chances of this occurring while one is in the Void are more or less the same as with every other level and are thus incredibly rare. ===Exits=== Random acts of no-clipping are the only known type of exit from the '''Parallel'''; these no-clips have a high chance of sending one through the [[The Void|Void]] and to a random level of the Backrooms. However, it is believed that no-clipping in the '''Parallel''' also has an extremely small chance of sending one to [[The Broken]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial='''Open Author Info'''|final='''Close Author Info'''|body= '''Author:''' [[User:Forknife468152|Forknife]] {{User:Forknife468152/Pages}} }}{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Nunito'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Libre Baskerville'); }} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Anomalous Levels]] [[Category:Dangerous]]
